commands.review_summary:
Adds an unsigned iOS build workflow and a Debian/Ubuntu shell helper that downloads an unsigned archive, converts it to an IPA, and launches AltServer-Linux-PyScript for local iPhone install.

PR surface: Config +125, Other +340. Total +465 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this PR adds a new local install workflow. The missing proof is after-change real behavior evidence for the Debian/Ubuntu plus iPhone helper path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer security and product review before repair, because the blocker is whether OpenClaw core should ship a privileged third-party sideloading helper and how that tool should be trusted.

target.title: scripts: add local iOS install helper via AltServer-Linux
target.body:
Adds a helper script for installing the unsigned OpenClaw iOS build onto a local iPhone from a Debian/Ubuntu machine.

What it does:
- Downloads the unsigned `.xcarchive` artifact from the GitHub Actions unsigned-build workflow (or accepts a local artifact).
- Converts the `.xcarchive` into an `.ipa`.
- Clones a configurable fork of `AltServer-Linux-PyScript`.
- Launches AltServer-Linux-PyScript so the user can sign and install the app with their own Apple ID over USB or Wi-Fi.

Apple ID credentials are prompted interactively by AltServer; they are never stored or passed as command-line arguments.

Notes:
- Requires a free Apple Account. Apps installed this way must be refreshed every 7 days (same as AltStore).
- The default AltServer fork is `SnoutFirst/AltServer-Linux-PyScript`; users can override with `ALTSERVER_REPO`.

Related: the unsigned-build workflow in #95917 produces the artifact consumed by this script.
target.author: snoutfirst
